title: ctenitis croftii
intro: Ctenitis croftii, also known as Croft's ctenitis, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Ctenitis croftii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Ctenitis croftii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
